covered_report_window: Immediate actionable window: Monday, June 22, 2026 after the U.S. market close, plus Tuesday, June 23, 2026 before the U.S. market open. The run occurred during the U.S. trading session, so later Tuesday after-close reporters are excluded.
market_context_summary: U.S. equities were modestly softer intraday with QQQ weaker than SPY, while investors watched U.S.-Iran peace-talk headlines, oil-price movement, Thursday PCE inflation data, and a light earnings calendar. The window was sparse: no confirmed notable Monday after-close reporters and two company-confirmed Tuesday before-open reporters qualified.

ranked_companies.timing: Tuesday, June 23, 2026 before U.S. market open; results before 8:30 AM ET call
ranked_companies.expected_driver: FY27 rental-growth tone; dollar utilization; FCF
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: The consensus leans up because Q3 showed sequential rental-revenue improvement, raised FY26 rental-growth midpoint, strong free cash flow and buyback support, but the stock has already rerated and needs constructive FY27 demand and capex commentary.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Company confirmed a June 23 results call and pre-call release; Q3 rental revenue grew 2.6%, full-year rental revenue guidance was narrowed/raised to 2%-3%, and free cash flow was guided around $2 billion.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Upside comes from stable or improving dollar utilization, continued mega-project demand, local non-residential stabilization, controlled capex, and reaffirmed shareholder-return capacity.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Downside comes from margin/cost pressure, a FY27 guide that implies slowing rental growth, higher capex with weaker FCF conversion, or the stock fading after its recent rally.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Current listed options implied-move data was not reliably available in accessible sources; IEX bars show SUNB rose from about $71.84 on May 11 to $83.52 intraday June 22, increasing reversal risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Analyst views are mixed-to-positive but not a clean upside signal: public consensus snapshots show Buy/Moderate Buy ratings but average price targets clustered near or below the current price, with upside mainly from higher-end targets.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: Pass A +36 / reversal 38; Pass B +29 / reversal 40; Pass C +32 / reversal 39. All three leaned up; no material disagreement.

ranked_companies.timing: Tuesday, June 23, 2026 before U.S. market open; 12:00 PM ET webcast
ranked_companies.expected_driver: Q4 fee revenue; FY27 demand tone
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: The consensus mildly leans up because Q3 fee revenue grew across all solutions and Q4 guidance brackets consensus, but the market likely needs FY2027 client-demand confidence rather than just an in-line Q4.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Company confirmed a before-open release; management guided Q4 FY26 fee revenue to $730-$750 million and diluted EPS of $1.34-$1.40, while public calendars list EPS around $1.38-$1.39.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Upside comes from fee revenue above the guide midpoint, resilient Executive Search and Consulting demand, stable adjusted EBITDA margin, and constructive FY2027 tone.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Downside comes from softer client decision cycles, weak Consulting backlog consumption, margin pressure from compensation costs, or a merely in-line quarter with cautious guidance.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Current options implied-move data was not reliably available in accessible sources; IEX bars show KFY pulled back from its June highs into the event, reducing but not eliminating reversal risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Benzinga shows the latest Truist update maintained a Buy rating with an $80 target on June 9, but broad upside is modest because the stock trades near recent target ranges.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: Pass A +30 / reversal 34; Pass B +22 / reversal 38; Pass C +26 / reversal 36. Two Lean Up and one high-Neutral; mild disagreement kept confidence capped.

qualified_names.why: Only name with absolute direction score above 30, reversal risk at or below 40, low pass disparity, and adequate evidence quality.

excluded_high_reversal_or_weak_names.reason: Direction score of 26 is below the >30 allocation threshold; setup is mild because consensus already sits inside management's Q4 guide.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice. The hypothetical same-day research basket assigns 60% to SUNB and leaves 40% unallocated rather than forcing a weaker idea.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: Pass A, Pass B, and Pass C all identified rental-growth and free-cash-flow tone as the key event driver and all landed at Lean Up.
detailed_company_sections.pass_agreement: Tight agreement with modest scoring spread; all passes leaned up but capped conviction because of recent rerating and capex/margin sensitivity.
detailed_company_sections.recommendation: Lean Up for research prioritization, not a strong directional call.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: SUNB can react positively if FY27 demand, dollar utilization, and free-cash-flow commentary confirm that Q3's sequential improvement was durable.
detailed_company_sections.what_market_expects: A pre-open Q4/FY26 release before the 8:30 AM ET call, with the market focused on whether the Q3 2%-3% FY26 rental-revenue growth outlook carries into a constructive FY27 setup.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_up: Rental revenue above plan, stronger General Tool utilization, steady Specialty growth, mega-project wins, controlled capex, and visible buyback/free-cash-flow support.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_down: A softer FY27 demand outlook, cost inflation, higher capex without FCF upside, lower dollar utilization, or a sell-the-news reaction after the June rally.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Low-to-moderate. The setup is supported by company data, but the stock has rerated materially and public analyst targets are not uniformly above the current price.
detailed_company_sections.reddit_social_consumer_sentiment_read: Sparse direct Reddit discussion; no strong retail crowding signal found. Customer-facing chatter was not strong enough to influence scoring.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning_read: Current implied-move data was not reliably accessible. Recent IEX price action showed a strong advance from May into June, so positioning risk was treated as a reversal-risk input.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Mixed-positive analyst backdrop: Buy/Moderate Buy consensus snapshots exist, but average price targets near the current price reduce the upside signal.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Constructive Q4 with steady FY27 commentary yields a modest positive reaction.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Management signals stronger FY27 rental growth and FCF conversion while keeping capex disciplined, leading to upside beyond the event move.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: The market focuses on cost pressure, weaker General Tool utilization, or capex-heavy growth and fades the stock despite acceptable headline numbers.
detailed_company_sections.final_directional_judgment: Lean Up, score 32, reversal risk 39, Medium confidence.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Included at 60% of the research basket because it cleared the score, reversal-risk, disparity, and evidence-quality filters; 40% remains unallocated.
detailed_company_sections.disparity_effect: Low disparity supported conviction quality, but the recent rerating kept the allocation below a full basket.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: The passes agreed on a mild positive setup but differed on whether it cleared the threshold for a real edge; the final average stayed Lean Up but close to Neutral.
detailed_company_sections.pass_agreement: Two passes were Lean Up and one was high-Neutral, mainly because Q4 consensus already matches management's prior guide.
detailed_company_sections.recommendation: Lean Up, but not allocation-priority quality.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: KFY has a mild upside path if Q4 fee revenue and FY27 demand commentary exceed a consensus bar that already sits near the prior guide midpoint.
detailed_company_sections.what_market_expects: A before-open Q4 FY26 release, Q4 EPS around $1.38-$1.39, and fee revenue near the $730-$750 million management guide.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_up: Better fee revenue, strength in Executive Search / Professional Search, stable adjusted EBITDA margin, and upbeat FY27 client-demand commentary.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_down: A cautious FY27 demand read, slowing Consulting activity, margin pressure from compensation costs, or no upside to the already-known Q4 guide.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Low-to-moderate. The stock pulled back into the event and valuation is not stretched, but an in-line quarter can still disappoint if forward commentary is cautious.
detailed_company_sections.reddit_social_consumer_sentiment_read: Very limited retail/social attention; no meaningful Reddit or consumer sentiment edge identified.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning_read: Current implied move was not reliably accessible. Recent IEX bars show a pullback from early-June highs, which lowers sell-the-news risk but does not create a squeeze setup.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Latest public analyst color from Benzinga shows Truist maintained Buy with an $80 target on June 9; the signal is supportive but not decisive.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Q4 lands near guidance and FY27 tone is stable, producing a small positive or muted reaction.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Fee revenue clears the high end of guidance and management points to improving client demand, lifting confidence in FY27 earnings growth.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: The quarter is merely in-line and forward commentary shows slower project starts or consulting/backlog consumption, pulling the stock lower.
detailed_company_sections.final_directional_judgment: Lean Up, score 26, reversal risk 36, Medium confidence.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Excluded because the final direction score was below the >30 allocation threshold and the edge is mild.
detailed_company_sections.disparity_effect: Disparity was still low, but the one high-Neutral pass pulled the consensus closer to Neutral and reduced allocation priority.

disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
